I found some very strange behavior where using dill.dump to store a Sympy lambdified expression causes built-in functions such as min, max, abs, etc. to be replaced with Numpy functions of the same name. 

    from sympy import symbols, lambdify
    import dill, inspect

    def check_if_builtin(func):
        try:
            file = inspect.getsourcefile(func) #will throw TypeError for builtin
            return file
        except TypeError:
            return True

    

    dill.settings['recurse'] = True #without this option, throws PicklingError

    a, b, c = symbols("a b c")
    expr = a + b + c
    lambda_expr = lambdify([a, b, c], expr)

    print(check_if_builtin(min))

    dill.dump(lambda_expr, open('test.p', 'wb'))

    print(check_if_builtin(min))

returns:

    True
    C:\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\numpy\core\fromnumeric.py

Naturally this breaks my code and I'm currently using a hackish fix to replace the builtins after pickling. Here is my setup:

Python 3.5.1
Windows 10 64-bit
dill: 0.2.5
sympy: 0.7.6.1
numpy: 1.11.0

issue_title: pathos has problem with parallel python and some modules
issue_body:
I am using pathos for my project and it works nicely for multiprocessing. However for parallel python I've got a problem - the system hangs on job submission.  With some investigation I've found an easy way to reproduce the problem using standard example sum_primesX.py.   It is enough  to add 'readline' module into the list of modules to be imported and system hangs at submission:

replace :

    jobs = [(input, job_server.submit(sum_primes, (input, ), (isprime, ),
            ("math", ))) for input in inputs]

with

     jobs = [(input, job_server.submit(sum_primes, (input, ), (isprime, ),
            ("math", "readline")) for input in inputs]


Is it a known issue?  Is there any solution?

issue_title: idea-intransitive is broken in intellIj. Remove idea-intransitive flag.
issue_body:
Intellij Project generated with idea  --idea-intransitive flag, do no compile. 

Intellij Project generated with idea --idea-intransitive flag, do no compile.

Prior to Round Engine Refactor,

./pants goal idea used to depend on goal 'jar'. 
However, it got removed somewhere in between Round Engine.
As jar is no longer run before idea-gen https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/ide_gen.py#L244 returns empty.

--idea-intransitive flag depended on the products of jar phase.
--idea-intransitive did not pull sources for internal dependencies under Source Folders.
It added the source-jars and jar products of 'jar' phase as module dependency.
This would save a significant time when you compile/make a project depending on several other projects in the same repo. 

Post Round Engine, internal-libs were empty and hence compiling project created with --idea-intransitive failed on finding internal dependencies.

This fix will remove the idea-transitive flag. I created a follow up bug to fix it

#417

issue_title: Automatically introduce dependencies upon request
issue_body:
In experiments, around 25% of Steps (during execution of `./pants list ::`) experience a situation where all of their deps have already been computed in the ProductGraph, but because the Step hasn't declared the dep yet, the local dict of Step dependencies doesn't contain the dep. So they exit, and they hit this case: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/c2cd962bc4758a728a2fe30fe951e0ab49f5b433/src/python/pants/engine/scheduler.py#L744-L745 ... meaning that they are immediately eligible to run again.

To handle this, one approach would be to drop the `dependencies` list from `Waiting`, and instead track requested dependencies in the StepContext. When a Node called `StepContext.get`, you'd determine whether the dependency was already declared; if it wasn't, you'd record it. Then you'd eagerly execute a context-specific lookup for the dep (ie, product_graph.get for single/multithreaded or Storage.get for multiprocess). This would avoid a roundtrip through the scheduler and an additional Step being created.

issue_title: No SplunkDestinationConfiguration in AWS::KinesisFirehose::DeliveryStream?
issue_body:
**Description:**

Original StackOverflow Post: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49784126/automate-creation-of-kinesis-firehose-delivery-stream-splunk-destination-with

I have successfully setup a Kinesis Stream to send Data to Splunk by using the Splunk Add-on for Amazon Kinesis (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3719/).

I have successfully setup a Kinesis Stream to send Data to Splunk by using the Splunk Add-on for Amazon Kinesis (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3719/).

I followed the procedure described in the official documentation: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/firehose/latest/dev/create-destination.html#create-destination-splunk

All works well and fine. Data is succesfully sent from Kinesis stream into Splunk.

The problem is that now to use this in production we need to create a SAM (https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model) to automate the deployment of the Delivery Stream.

Currently my yaml template looks like (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/with-kinesis-example-use-app-spec.html):

```
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31
Resources:
  KinesisStream:
    Type: AWS::Kinesis::Stream
    Properties:
      ShardCount: 2
      Name: my-kinesis-stream-name

  ProcessKinesisRecords:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Properties:
      Handler: handler
      Runtime: runtime
      Policies:
       - AWSLambdaExecute # Managed Policy
       - Version: '2012-10-17' # Policy Document
         Statement:
           - Effect: Allow
             Action:
               - dynamodb:GetItem
               - dynamodb:Scan
               - dynamodb:Query
             Resource: !Join [ "", [ "arn:aws:dynamodb:", !Ref "AWS::Region", ":", !Ref "AWS::AccountId", ":table/TABLE_NAME" ] ]
      CodeUri: CodePackage.zip
      Events:
        Stream:
          Type: Kinesis
          Properties:
            Stream: !GetAtt KinesisStream.Arn
            BatchSize: 25
            StartingPosition: TRIM_HORIZON
```

This template works fine when I deploy it with CloudFormation and it successfully creates the Kinesis Stream, Lambda function and Roles.

How can I extend this template to also create a Firehose Delivery Stream using the SplunkDestinationConfiguration?

Here's the Firehose Configuration I want to Automate:

SAM Firehose Configuration

I've found the following piece of documentation in AWS (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-kinesisfirehose-deliverystream.html) which points me towards adding the following block into my **SAM template**:

```
Type: "AWS::KinesisFirehose::DeliveryStream"
Properties: 
  DeliveryStreamName: String
  DeliveryStreamType: String
  ElasticsearchDestinationConfiguration:
    ElasticsearchDestinationConfiguration
  ExtendedS3DestinationConfiguration:
    ExtendedS3DestinationConfiguration
  KinesisStreamSourceConfiguration:
    KinesisStreamSourceConfiguration
  RedshiftDestinationConfiguration:
    RedshiftDestinationConfiguration
  S3DestinationConfiguration:
    S3DestinationConfiguration
```

But notice that there's **no way to specify a "SplunkDestinationConfiguration"** at least it isn't explained in the documentation for SAM. Can anyone help in understanding whether this is supported by SAM currently and if not how can one accomplish that?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create a SAM template that deploys a Simple Lambda Function and set the source as Kinesis Stream as described in: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/with-kinesis-example-use-app-spec.html
2. Create a Splunk Trial Server from AWS Marketplace https://aws.amazon.com/marketplace/pp/B00PUXWXNE
3. Try to follow the explanation in the documentation at https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-kinesisfirehose-deliverystream.html to AUTOMATE the creation of the Kinesis Firehose Delivery Stream with Splunk Destination.

**Observed result:**

It currently doesn't seem to be possible to specify SplunkDestinationConfiguration in the SAM yaml template to automate the creation of the Kinesis Firehose Data Delivery Stream.

**Expected result:**

I would like to be able to create the Delivery Stream from my SAM template and CloudFormation automatically.

issue_title: Starting a new project and some day 1 requirements are missing, guidance?
issue_body:
Been playing around with SAM for about 6 months and I really love it. I have my proof of concept done and realized there are some day 1 requirements for my projects missing.

1. CORS (easy to work around. Already implemented)
2. Authorizers with Cognito (Can implement custom authorization inside the code)
3. Custom Domains (can work around for a few months)
4. Cache TTL (can implement a caching service or live without for a few months)
5. API Key (can use cloud formation with some code workaround)

These are all covered in #248 but I am between a rock and a hard place. My go live date is July and I have to make a decision. Do I wait for the items in #248 and hope that they make it in or do I just assume these features won't make it in time. This will force me to (painfully) write hacks in my code to get everything to work.

I tried to get a sense of timeline from @jfuss comment on Jan 8 when he said "All of these (in the issue) are being prioritize with high priority in the team and are going to be the first set of things we will address this year."

Can you please provide either an ETA or tell us your release plan? Will it be one large release or will it be minor releases? Any insight will be appreciated.

Again keep up the great work.

issue_title: tiff read/write problem
issue_body:
Some code of mine which is writing and reading tiffs via imageio occasionally crashes as it fails to read tiff files that have been written by imageio's tiff writer. I suspect this is due to this bug fix in tifffile which has been fixed: "2016.02.22 Write 8 bytes double tag values using offset if necessary (bug fix)." If I use a recent or current version of tifffile I don't see this problem anymore, so for the moment my workaround is to use tifffile directly.
As the version of tifffile used by imageio (2014.08.24) is getting quite out of date (Chistoph Gohlke's latest version is 12 releases newer), would it be possible to simply update the version used by imageio to the current version?

Is there any workaround? Any way to get the details for a specific tag?

issue_title: Python - default matrix TITLE attribute is some funny character that is causing problems
issue_body:
The default matrix TITLE attribute is some funny character that is causing problems.  In order to read our matrices into the OMX Viewer and VISUM, we had to override the TITLE after the matrix was created.  We added the following bold line:

import openmatrix as omx
outfile = omx.openFile(os.path.join(dir_data, 'transit_od_demand_new_' + per + '.omx'), 'w')
outfile['metro'] = od_matrix[i+1][0]
outfile.createMapping('taz', taz_labels)
**outfile['metro'].attrs.TITLE = 'metro'**
outfile.close()

issue_title: Lazify objects can't be json_encode'd
Change shared memory in container when creating a container via docker Client
issue_body:
The current version of docker allows the user to change the size of shared memory inside a container, via ```
docker run --shm-size
```
but the docker-py `Client.create_container()` does not support this yet. 

This is bound to happen again as docker adds more features, so maybe you should consider taking *args into `Client.create_container()` which would be parsed directly into the `docker run` command.

issue_title: Simplify `nifgen` event configuration
issue_body:
[Zen of Python](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0020/#id3):

> Simple is better than complex.

NI-FGEN provides a lot of configurability for triggers and events. You can change polarity, play with pulse width, set them as level instead of pulses... It's extremely rare to configure these and it's also not supported by the "latest and greatest" NI-FGEN device family 54x3.

For those reasons, we should remove the properties for all events:

* `event_level_active_level`
* `event_output_behavior`
* `event_pulse_polarity`
* `event_pulse_width`
* `event_pulse_width_units`
* `event_toggle_initial_state`

Keep:

* `event_output_terminal`
* `event_live_status`
* `event_latched_status`

Debate:

* `event_delay` and `event_delay_units`
   * `nidcpower.Session.measure_complete_event_delay`:  If we keep this, should we keep the `nifgen` _event_delay` attributes? What about `event_delay_units` which applies only to `nifgen`? What about the fact that in `nidcpower` the attribute is `AttributeViReal64TimeDeltaSeconds`

issue_title: Use eth-hash.keccak instead of eth-utils.keccak
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

`eth_utils.keccak` enables hashes of multiple types, including text strings and hex strings. This comes at a small performance cost, but py-trie uses keccak heavily so is fairly sensitive to these costs. py-trie only ever submits `bytes` for hashing, so the performance penalty is unnecessary.

### How to fix it

- Replace all usages of `eth_utils.keccak` with `eth_hash.auto.keccak`.
- Add eth-hash as a dependency, and eth-hash[pycryptodome] as a testing dependency.

issue_title: Better gas price engine algorithm
issue_body:
Depends on #494 

### What needs to be done?

The algorithm used by https://ethgasstation.info/ is pretty solid for determining gas prices.  Lets implement something similar

### How can it be done?

Implement a new gas price engine (see #494 ) that uses the following algorithm to compute gas prices.

The algorithm will be configured with the following parameters:

- Let `N` be the number of historical blocks that should be sampled.
- Let `P` be a number between [0-1] which represents the probability for gas price estimation.  0 meaning 0% probability and 1 meaning 100% probability.
- Let `D` be the desired maximum wait time in seconds for the transaction to be mined.

We then compute the following information by fetching data from the blockchain.

- Let `T` be the *average* block time for the last `N` blocks.
- Let `C` be the set of miners from the last `N` blocks.
- For each miner `c` from `C`:
  - Let `c.totalBlocks` be the number of blocks that `c` mined in the last `N` blocks.
  - Let `c.minPrice` be the lowest gas price accepted by that miner across all of the blocks mined in the last `N` blocks.

With this information we can now compute a gas price such that a transaction is likely to be mined within `D` seconds with probability `P`.  The formula for this is as follows.

issue_title: Rearranging the module hierarchy
issue_body:
For a major release, maybe a v1.0.0, it would be a good opportunity to rearrange the package hierarchy to follow [PEP-0423](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0423/#avoid-deep-nesting). This should coincide with other backwards-incompatible changes (e.g. restructuring the \*mrs classes) so users don't have to refactor too many times.

E.g.,

```
delphin.tokens
delphin.derivations
delphin.tdl
delphin.tfs
delphin.itsdb
delphin.mrs
delphin.eds
delphin.dmrs
delphin.dm
delphin.semi
delphin.vpm
delphin.ace
delphin.http
```

I'm not sure what to do about codecs, though. Should there be a third level (e.g. `delphin.io.simplemrs`), or should they be classes/functions under the corresponding module (`delphin.mrs.SimpleMRSCodec`)

And maybe some, but not too much, effort should be expended to maintain backward compatibility with deprecated import paths.

issue_title: Certain custom_multichannels entries produce warnings
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior If there are channel entries in a `custom_multichannels` configuration that are suffixes of another channel, `conda` prints a warning: ``` WARNING: The remote server could not find the noarch directory for the requested channel with url: https://conda.anaconda.org/bioconda/label/deprecated/conda.anaconda.org/bioconda ``` Triggered by the prefix check at https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4.4.11/conda/models/channel.py#L413. Despite that warning, it looks like the correct channel URLs are used since subsequent operations succeed. ### Steps to Reproduce ``` $ docker run --rm -it continuumio/miniconda3 root@f5d1379434b5:/# conda update -qy conda >/dev/null root@f5d1379434b5:/# conda --version conda 4.4.11 root@f5d1379434b5:/# cat > ~/.condarc <<'EOF' > custom_multichannels: > bioconda-repro: > - bioconda > - conda-forge > - defaults > - bioconda/label/deprecated > - bioconda/label/broken > EOF root@f5d1379434b5:/# conda search --info bioconda-repro::julia=0.3.11 Loading channels: | WARNING: The remote server could not find the noarch directory for the requested channel with url: https://conda.anaconda.org/bioconda/label/deprecated/conda.anaconda.org/bioconda It is possible you have given conda an invalid channel. Please double-check your conda configuration using `conda config --show`. If the requested url is in fact a valid conda channel, please request that the channel administrator create `noarch/repodata.json` and associated `noarch/repodata.json.bz2` files, even if `noarch/repodata.json` is empty. $ mkdir noarch $ echo '{}' > noarch/repodata.json $ bzip2 -k noarch/repodata.json done julia 0.3.11 0 -------------- file name : julia-0.3.11-0.tar.bz2 name : julia version : 0.3.11 build string: 0 build number: 0 size : 28.6 MB arch : x86_64 constrains : () platform : Platform.linux license : MIT subdir : linux-64 url : https://conda.anaconda.org/bioconda/label/deprecated/linux-64/julia-0.3.11-0.tar.bz2 md5 : b8afb4685ec0cd983f78f8dc66fe009f dependencies: ``` ## Expected Behavior Don't compose malformed URLs, don't output warning. ## Environment Information
`conda info`

``` active environment : None user config file : /root/.condarc populated config files : /root/.condarc conda version : 4.4.11 conda-build version : not installed python version : 3.6.4.final.0 base environment : /opt/conda (writable) channel URLs : https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/main/linux-64 https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/main/noarch https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/linux-64 https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/free/noarch https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/r/linux-64 https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/r/noarch https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/linux-64 https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/pro/noarch package cache : /opt/conda/pkgs /root/.conda/pkgs envs directories : /opt/conda/envs /root/.conda/envs platform : linux-64 user-agent : conda/4.4.11 requests/2.18.4 CPython/3.6.4 Linux/4.14.19-1-MANJARO debian/8 glibc/2.19 UID:GID : 0:0 netrc file : None offline mode : False ```

``` ==> /root/.condarc <== custom_multichannels: bioconda-repro: ['bioconda', 'conda-forge', 'defaults', 'bioconda/label/deprecated', 'bioconda/label/broken'] ```

issue_title: conda packages dependencies: lack of update, problems after update
issue_body:
Hi,

We have a channel where we distribute our packages (https://anaconda.org/acellera). We find conda a great platform to distribute our software, but we find the updates rather troublesome much of the times, specially related with dependencies.

**Issue number 1:** When doing `conda update htmd -c acellera`, our main package, we notice many times that the dependencies are not updated. We know there is a flag, `--update-dependencies`, specifically related with this, but we trust the default to be working. Is this not the case? In this end, we sometimes recommend people to use the `conda update --all`, which I've read somewhere that it should be used carefully. We currently also implemented a way of having specific versions associated with the package instead of keeping it to the latest, but it doesn't seem like it has improved the behaviour much. 

**Issue number 2:** Alternatively to the behaviour in Issue 1, the package is updated, as shown by `conda list`, but the Python module still behaves like the old one, suggesting that there may be a conflict between the older installation and the new one.

**Issue number 3:** Sometimes, we change our package, and create a new dependency for it. For new installations, everything works fine, but for installations that are updated to a new version using `conda update`, for some people and more often than we wished for, the package cannot de imported, like it was missing. Despite that, the command `conda list` reports it.

Many times, we are left clueless about these issues, and all we can suggest to our users is to reinstall conda, which normally fixes the issues. Do all these issues mean that `conda update` cannot be trusted? Asking our users to always be installing conda is very annoying.

We appreciate any feedback that could improve this issues.

João

issue_title: reindex() should have a higher default scan size
issue_body:
While trying to re-index an ES 1.7 index in preparation for ES 2.X, we hit some pretty bad performance issues (see https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/18253 and https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/issues/397).  Turns out this was caused by `reindex()` using the default `size` kwarg for the `scan()` method, which is 10.  Obviously the optimal value depends on many factors, but 10 is much too small for a reindex operation.  For us, passing in a value of 1000 sped up our reindex by an order of magnitude:

```
es_helpers.reindex(
    args.sourceCluster,
    args.sourceIndex,
    args.targetIndex,
    target_client=args.workerTargetClient,
    chunk_size=args.chunkSize,
    scroll=args.scroll,
    scan_kwargs={'size': args.searchQuerySize},
    bulk_kwargs=bulkArgs)
```  

Note that elasticsearch-core's 2.3 reindex method just increased their default from 100 to 1000 (https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/18340).  I'm proposing that elasticsearch-py does the same.

It would be nice to back-port this to the 1.X branch too, in order to facilitate the migration of indices from 1.X to 2.X.  We would not have been able to upgrade to 2.X using elasticsearch-py unless we made this fix.

issue_title: Question: support for multiple configurations?
issue_body:
I'm interested in experimenting with different sets of hooks, having different hooks run on my build system versus locally, etc. 

Straightforward ways this might be possible:
- ``` pre-commit run --yaml $PATH --all-files```
- ```PRE_COMMIT_YAML_PATH=$PATH git commit -m "example"```

A more advanced option would be something like adding a ```groups``` yaml key as a sibling of  ```id``` e.g.

```yaml
-   repo: git://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    sha: v0.4.2
    hooks:
    -   id: trailing-whitespace
        groups: [basic, advanced]
    -   id: flake8
        groups: [advanced]
```

You could then have ```pre-commit run --groups ...``` and perhaps ```pre-commit set-commit-groups ...```

I don't see anything like this discussed in the docs. Are there advanced/undocumented features that might support this? Is this functionality inline with pre-commit's project goals, or too complicated/promotes a workflow the project doesn't agree with?

  File "/home/ich/code/aiohttp/aiohttp/connector.py", line 386, in connect
    self._acquired.remove(placeholder)
KeyError: <aiohttp.connector._TransportPlaceholder object at 0x7f270bc78c88>
```

So, `placeholder` is added to `self._acquired` (line 379) and then removed again (line 386), but inbetween it has already been removed, hence the `KeyError`.

The culprit is `self._create_connection(req)` (line 382), and eventually `self._loop.create_connection()` (line 732), and then I'm losing track.

Anyway, a simple `if placeholder in self._acquired:` inserted in line 386 seems to fix the issue for me. Or it could be something wrong with my code, but then I still have no idea what it is I'm doing wrong. I'd appreciate any pointers what could cause this.

I can reproduce, but unfortunately not with simple, sharable code.

issue_title: Strange behavior using a notebook from the other side of the planet
issue_body:
I'm using a notebook server that's in the US while out of the country.  The client keeps popping up a dialog box with the following message:

    The notebook file has changed on disk since the last time we opened or saved it. Do you want to overwrite the file on disk with the version open here, or load the version on disk (reload the page)?

When I'm away from the notebook for a while, it looks like quite a few of them pop up, as I have to click repeatedly to make the dialog go away (and the dimmed background becomes less dim the more I click).

There are two issues.
 
 1. The notebook on disk hasn't changed.  I suspect something in the client-side code is comparing the current time to the server-side modification time of the notebook file.  If that's the case, I think that's a bug.
 1. The dialog box seems to pop up multiple times.  I'm not a UI person, so I might be wrong about this, but it sure seems like that's what's happening.

issue_title: Use the History API
issue_body:
At least for the dashboard/file system browser, it would be a much cleaner ("no flicker") experience if, when clicking through the file system on our dashboard, we used the [history API](http://diveintohtml5.info/history.html) and manipulated the DOM directly. We do use the contents API for the directory listing, so the dashboard is probably one of the easiest spots to use `history.pushState` and *only* update the view of the current directory.

`history.pushState`, `history.popState`, and `history.replaceState` are [all well supported across browsers](http://caniuse.com/#search=pushstate) now a days (and have been for a while).

Creating folders/files is fine. If they're named "trust", though, I get a "Method Not Allowed". I guess we can say Jupyter Notebook has trust issues.

issue_title: Add configurable transformer for kernel messages
issue_body:
Currently, kernel messages are passed straight through from the kernel to the user in https://github.com/jupyter/notebook/blob/d86714a0158f3ba021357b47b29f2124e36725ef/notebook/base/zmqhandlers.py. The server administrator may want some control about messages getting sent to the user through some transformation functions running on the web server.  The server administrator could then do things like:

* for security, strip out javascript display_data messages, or escape html messages
* to conserve client memory in the notebook models the client is using, strip out inline images, store the images on the server, and replace the images with URLs to fetch
* to not overwhelm the client, truncate long messages, or redirect messages to a server-side store and insert a message telling the user how to request the rest of the output

The zmq/websocket bridge is the one place that is not tainted by user code, so would be the place to insert such a transformation.  It would be great if there was a way to specify a custom transformation class from the command line as an option.

issue_title: Error with <validator type="dataset_metadata_in_file"/>
issue_body:
Dear Planemo maintainers,

I am testing a tool that makes use of a validator of type `dataset_metadata_in_file`. The idea is to let galaxy check whether a selected db key exists within "liftOver.loc":

```
<tool id="test" name="test" version="1.0">
    <command><![CDATA[
        echo "$input" > "$output"
    ]]></command>

    <inputs>

        <param format="bam" name="input" type="data" label="bam file file">
            <validator type="dataset_metadata_in_file"
                       filename="liftOver.loc"
                       metadata_name="dbkey"
                       metadata_column="0"
                       message="LiftOver mapping (chain file) is not available for the specified build." />
        </param>

    </inputs>
    
    <outputs>
        <data format="text" name="output" label="${tool.name} on ${on_string}"/>
    </outputs>
</tool>
```

When `planemo test` and `planemo serve` are initiated, it doesn't load the tool and gives the following error:
*galaxy.tools.toolbox.base ERROR 2016-05-28 13:46:33,556 Error reading tool configuration file from path: /path/to/err.xml.*

I figured out that this is because the file '/path/to/planemo-galaxy-instace/test-data/liftOver.loc' does not exist and is not automatically derived from '/path/to/test-data/liftOver.loc.sample'. 

---
The direectory tree looks like this:

```
├── err.xml
├── test-data
│   ├── aToB.over.chain
├── tool-data
│   └── liftOver.loc.sample
└── tool_dependencies.xml

```
---

I hope there is an easy solution for this. Let me know if you need more details,

Youri

issue_title: Make cache operations into coroutines
issue_body:
Cache operations involve shelling out to git. On Linux this works ok, but on Windows it's pretty slow, and that leads to a bunch of warnings like this when we run tests with `PYTHONASYNCIODEBUG=1`:

```
Executing <Task finished coro=<Main.do_sync() done, defined at
C:\Users\Jack\peru\peru\main.py:86> result=None created at
C:\Users\Jack\peru\third-party\asyncio\base_events.py:264> took
0.203 seconds
```

Making the cache operations coroutines instead of synchronous functions should unblock the main thread and fix these warnings. Maybe we could make an asynchronous wrapper around the existing class?

issue_title: Should event_loop fixture call asyncio.set_event_loop()?
issue_body:
I use pytest and pytest-asyncio to test a few Tornado applications. I had some trouble (see https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/issues/2324) with Tornado getting a different event loop from the one created by pytest-asyncio's `event_loop` fixture.

This can be fixed by overriding Tornado's default testing behavior to use the "current" loop, rather than creating a new one. The issue is that pytest-asyncio does not call `asyncio.set_event_loop` with the loop it creates in the `event_loop` fixture – without which, there is no "current" loop.

I'd like to propose change the `event_loop` fixture to look like this:

```python
@fixture
def event_loop(request):
    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
    asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
    yield loop
    loop.close()
    asyncio.set_event_loop(None)
```

How does that sound? I'm happy to submit a PR, but I wanted to get feedback on the idea first.

issue_title: Impolite service termination
issue_body: In the finalizer for the plugin, the [current termination logic](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-services/blob/b55325985496b7a0fec13f9bf55e12915c83215e/pytest_services/service.py#L72-L79) is to first kill, then terminate the proc. On Windows, these two calls are the same, but on Unix, these calls should be reversed. `terminate` is the more polite operation, asking the child to shutdown cleanly, whereas `kill` is equivalent to `kill -9` and will cause the child to simply stop without cleaning up. This approach means that a complex service that starts its own child services will stop but leave these other processes running.

issue_title: Page promotions ordering
issue_body:
promotions.views.PageDetailView  has a bug.

    def post(self, request, **kwargs):
        """
        When called with a post request, try and get 'promo[]' from
        the post data and use it to reorder the page content blocks.
        """
        data = dict(request.POST).get('promo[]')
        self._save_page_order(data)
        return HttpResponse(status=200)
causes an Exception. POST is a QueryDict({'promo': [1, 2, 3...] })

this works:
    def post(self, request, **kwargs):
        """
        When called with a post request, try and get 'promo[]' from
        the post data and use it to reorder the page content blocks.
        """
        data = request.POST.getlist('promo')
        data = {data[x]: x for x in range(len(data))}

        self._save_page_order(data)
        return HttpResponse(status=200)

issue_title: Alpine Linux (for Docker images)
issue_body:
Support for Alpine Linux has been mentioned in the past on various repositories. It's desirable because the [Docker image for Alpine](https://hub.docker.com/_/alpine/) is under 5MB in size, compared to over 100MB for Debian 8, which is the current base for the `dotnet` images. Docker are looking to migrate all their official library images to Alpine.

The blocker for .NET Core has been the availability of the core dependencies on Alpine, of which the last outstanding one was LTTng, which is now in the APK testing repository for Alpine 3.5.

I'm not an expert on building .NET Core, CLI, etc, but if anybody fancies having a go at creating an Alpine build, it should be possible.

Looking at the Linux build instructions for CoreCLR, all the APT packages mentioned are available now, at least in Alpine's edge/testing repository, if not in edge/main:

APT | APK
---- | ----
cmake | cmake
llvm-3.5 | llvm 
clang-3.5 | clang (appears to be 3.8)
lldb-3.6 | lldb (in [edge/testing](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/testing/x86_64/lldb))
lldb-3.6-dev | lldb-dev (in [edge/testing](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/testing/x86_64/lldb-dev))
libunwind8 | libunwind (in edge/main)
libunwind8-dev | libunwind-dev (in edge/main)
gettext | gettext
libicu-dev | icu-dev
liblttng-ust-dev | lttng-ust-dev (in [edge/testing](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/testing/x86_64/lttng-ust-dev))
libcurl4-openssl-dev | curl-dev
libssl-dev | openssl-dev
uuid-dev | util-linux-dev (seems to be where the `uuid.h` file lives)

And here's a list of the deps from the `dotnet` image with their Alpine equivalents.

APT package | APK package
-------------- | ---------------
libc6 | [libc6-compat](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/libc6-compat)
libcurl3 | [libcurl](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/libcurl)
libgcc1 | [libgcc](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/libgcc)
libgssapi-krb5-2 | [krb5-libs](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/krb5-libs)
libicu52 | [icu-libs](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/icu-libs)
liblttng-ust0 | [lttng-ust-dev](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/testing/x86_64/lttng-ust-dev)
libssl1.0.0 | [libssl1.0](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/libssl1.0)
libstdc++6 | [libstdc++](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/libstdc++)
libunwind8 | [libunwind](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/libunwind)
libuuid1 | [libuuid](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/libuuid)
zlib1g | [zlib](https://pkgs.alpinelinux.org/package/edge/main/x86_64/zlib)

Right, this is where somebody points out that the only reason these dependencies have made it into Alpine is that a .NET Core team member ported them all, for precisely this reason, and I've wasted the time spent on this issue. :disappointed:

issue_title: Renew command does not only update "about to expire" certificates.
issue_body:
As far as I understand `./letsencrypt-auto renew` should try to renew only certificates that are about to expire.
When during the renew succeeds for some domains, but fails for others, re-running it will also try to renew the certificates that already have been successfully renewed.

Basically when running the command X times there should never be a situation where rate limit error is thrown for a certificate that has already been renewed.
Example, my certificate at `https://bugtracker.befound.nl` was renewed, but when I (re)run the renew command I get this:
````
2016-02-23 10:55:45,925:WARNING:letsencrypt.cli:Attempting to renew cert from /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/bugtracker.befound.nl.conf produced an unexpected error: urn:acme:error:rateLimited :: There were too many requests of a given type :: Error creating new cert :: Too many certificates already issued for: befound.nl. Skipping.

issue_title: Update tox/instructions
issue_body:
As pointed out by @TheBoegl, our [instructions for submitting a PR](https://letsencrypt.readthedocs.org/en/latest/contributing.html#submitting-a-pull-request) are misleading. Step 5 says:

> Run `tox` to run the entire test suite including coverage. Fix any errors.

Currently, running `tox` runs the following tests: py{26,27,33,34,35},py{26,27}-oldest,cover,lint.

Many of these tests such as tests for 5 python versions as well of the *-oldest tests which require an older version OpenSSL are not likely to run successfully on a user's system.

We should either:

1. Update `envlist` in `tox.ini` so that only a subset of the current tests are run. In most cases, I think cover, lint, and maybe a py3x test would be sufficient. We should, however, continue running all of our tests in Travis.
2. Update the developer instructions to explain that `ERROR: InterpreterNotFound:` for the py* tests or compilation failures for the *-oldest tests do not indicate a problem with your PR and you can continue with submitting your PR.

issue_title: external_ip segment hangs the shell because of network timeout
issue_body:
Helluu!

I experience that the segment `external_ip` is not working good if there is no Internet connection -- it hangs the powerline-daemon for 10 seconds.

# The setup
I have configured correctly powerline for `tmux` and for `zsh`. The cofiguration for tmux uses the `external_ip` segment. 

# The problem
When no internet connection is available, the IP segment at

`/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/powerline/segments/common/net.py:_external_ip()`

uses 

`/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/powerline/lib/url.py:urllib_read()`

which in turn uses 

```python
urlopen(timeout=10)
```

What now happens is that the powerline-daemon freezes for 10 seconds. If you during this time create a new shell, it will hang too as the powerline daemon is busy. If you press enter in an open shell, it hangs too, as the shell status can't be printed either (uses powerline).

Maybe add a configuration parameter cold be added to `external_ip` to set the network timout setting to something lower than 10s? 

What do you think? 

Having shells freezing because of no Internet connection is simply not good!

issue_title: BigDL support Alpine 3.6+
issue_body:
/lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 is missing in Alpine 3.6+ 

this is trying to support spark on K8s, which use spark images created from Alpine os.

The main caveat to note is that it does use **musl** libc instead of glibc and friends, so certain software might run into issues depending on the depth of their libc requirements. However, most software doesn't have an issue with this, so this variant is usually a very safe choice. See this Hacker News comment thread for more discussion of the issues that might arise and some pro/con comparisons of using Alpine-based images.

issue_title: Senza traffic should wait for stack to be ready to change traffic
issue_body:
### Current Behaviour

When running change traffic `senza traffic` on stack recently deployed. The status code of the stack is not ready (`(CRETE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE`) and Senza fails trying to update it.
### Cause of the Problem

It is not possible to update CF stack when it's status code is not in `(CRETE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE` (or we should not change when it is not in one of those status code).
### Expected Behaviour

Senza should wait the stack to get in a final status code if the current status of the stack is `(CREATE|UPDATE)_IN_PROGRESS` and ending up in some good state `(CREATE|UPDATE)_COMPLETE`.

issue_title: Problem with => within Horn clauses definitions
issue_body:
I have different behavior concerning the use of logical implication =>when defining a Horn predicate.
Since I have hard time computing a minimal working example I am sharing the file as is. Sorry for the inconvenience.

I have two sets of files: test_ok and test_nok. test_ok should be proved valid, ie. an unsat result, while test_not is its negation and should be falsified, ie. a sat result.

For each of these files I have two versions: one (the invalid one) with the direct use of "a => b" to encode implication within the Horn predicate definition. And one (the valid one) where it is replaced by "(not a) or b".

I call the following shell command to try versions 4.3.1, 4.3.2 and 4.4.2 of z3

for f in test_*valid.smt2; do 
  echo $f; 
  echo z4.3.1: `z3 $f` $?; 
  echo z3-4.3.2 `z3-4.3.2 $f` $?; 
  echo z3-4.4.2 `z3-4.4.2 $f` $?; 
done

I obtain the following results:

The two files relying on (not a) or b perform similarly and provide the expected result.
test_nok_valid.smt2
z4.3.1: sat 0
z3-4.3.2 sat 0
z3-4.4.2 sat 0

test_ok_valid.smt2
z4.3.1: 124  --- the call fails and stop in the middle of its computation with an error code 124.
z3-4.3.2 unsat 0
z3-4.4.2 unsat 0

However when relying on "=>" the behavior completely diverge:

test_nok_invalid.smt2
z4.3.1: sat 0
z3-4.3.2 (error "query failed: interpreted body (=> (= (:var 1) Even) (and (mini_states__Even_handler_until (:var 3) (:var 1) (:var 17) (:var 16) (:var 15)) (= (:var 7) (:var 15)))) contains recursive predicate") unknown 0
z3-4.4.2 unsat 0

Here we have both sat/unsat/unknow (because of some strange recursive predicate complain)

test_ok_invalid.smt2
z4.3.1: 124
z3-4.3.2 (error "query failed: interpreted body (=> (= (:var 1) Even) (and (mini_states__Even_handler_until (:var 3) (:var 1) (:var 17) (:var 16) (:var 15)) (= (:var 7) (:var 15)))) contains recursive predicate") unknown 0
z3-4.4.2 unsat 0

Here we have a failure/unknown (because of the same recursive predicate complain)/unsat.

Note that z3 4.4.2 provide the unsat result both for a property P and its negation. 

[test_ok_valid.smt2.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/81509/test_ok_valid.smt2.txt)

issue_title: [refactor] Clean up the editor package
issue_body:
As the codemirror editor react component got merged together, some of the old cruft was kept for the sake of timeliness and stability. _However_, this left some warts that manifest themselves as either bugs or extra complexity that we can do away with:

* [ ] Additional props that we were allowing for configuration, which should just be set as codemirror options `props.options`.
  * [ ] `cursorBlinkRate`
  * [ ] `language` -- should switch to `mode`, being tackled by @mpacer 
  * [ ] `preserveScrollPosition` -- I'm not sure if/when we'd use this one (?)
* [ ] Switch tooltips to using `ReactDOM.createPortal` -- see https://github.com/nteract/nteract/blob/f41b58503325e844b1424a8ab29a4a71afadc102/packages/editor/src/index.js#L287
* [ ] `componentWillRecieveProps` has the proper (?) merging of new options into codemirror, whereas our constructor (and `componentDidMount`) have this weird mishmash of defaultOptions (by necessity for binding sake) and 
* [ ] The codemirror creates several subscriptions that it does not close, namely `keyupEvents`, `tip`s, and `completions`. I _think_ we should put these in the component's state and make sure to dispose of them on `componentDidUnmount`

Feel free to add more. 😋

issue_title: Add Flow typings to React component
issue_body:
We've got several React components stored under `src/notebook/components` in our codebase. These React components contain a `propType` property that defines the types of the different props the component takes. We'd like to use [Flow](https://flowtype.org/) to make these typings.

Pick one of the React components in our codebase and integrate Flow into it. [This](http://www.robinwieruch.de/the-soundcloud-client-in-react-redux-flow/#reactComponents) article provides good information on how to set up Flow and integrate it with React components.

issue_title: Issue on sending content with specific length, NETCONF delimiter gets split
issue_body:
When trying to send (to a Juniper device) a set of commands with a specific length ncclient crashes.
It seems that the netconf delimiter (`]]>]]>`) is being split while transporting.

DEBUG level logging output:

```
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.session:dispatching message to <ncclient.operations.rpc.RPCReplyListener object at 0x7fde1eb66ad0>: <rpc-reply xmlns="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" xmlns:junos="http://xml.juniper.net/junos/15.1R5/junos" xmlns:nc="urn:ietf:params:xml:ns:netconf:base:1.0" message-id="urn:uuid:ebf7759f-9a0d-4b90-a8ae-ac7818f68338">
DEBUG:ncclient.operations.rpc:Delivering to <ncclient.operations.third_party.juniper.rpc.ExecuteRpc object at 0x7fde25ab5e90>
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.ssh:Trying another round of parsing since there is still data
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.ssh:parsing netconf v1.0
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.ssh:EOF in transport thread
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.ssh:Broke out of main loop, error=SessionCloseError('Unexpected session close\nIN_BUFFER: `\n`',)
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.session:dispatching error to <ncclient.transport.session.NotificationHandler object at 0x7fde1eb66e10>
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.session:dispatching error to <jnpr.junos.device.DeviceSessionListener object at 0x7fde25f70d50>
DEBUG:ncclient.transport.session:dispatching error to <ncclient.operations.rpc.RPCReplyListener object at 0x7fde1eb66ad0>
```

While debugging, [this](https://github.com/ncclient/ncclient/blob/master/ncclient/transport/ssh.py#L135) seems to be the problematic code snippet:

```
        else:
            # handle case that MSG_DELIM is split over two chunks
            self._parsing_pos10 = buf.tell() - MSG_DELIM_LEN
            if self._parsing_pos10 < 0:
                self._parsing_pos10 = 0
```

This is located in `SSHSession._parse10()` which is called from `SSHSession.run()`. The latter reads message data and adds it into a buffer, for `_parse10()` to read. Then `_parse10()` tries to locate the 
NETCONF delimiter inside the buffer. If, however, the delimiter is split between two buffers it cannot locate it. I tried altering the code in `_parse10()` to move the buffer pointer in the end of the buffer, hoping that when `run()` loads the next chunk directly afterwards the delimiter will be complete and the
next execution of `_parse10()` will locate it correctly. I did not succeed though.

Any ideas?

To reproduce use the python code & input file provided in this [gist](https://gist.github.com/sergafts/fd0d036c2895e82ff66c005d19c4deca)

issue_title: Cannot execute shell commands via cli.Client
issue_body:
Python's `subprocess` module offers a very handy function under Python 3, `run`. It's used much like `cli.Client`. One of the few reasons I'm not currently using it is that it's unavailable on Python 2. Interest in Python 2 is also why I backported(ish) `pulp_smash.cli.CompletedProcess`. Here's a usage example:

```python
>>> subprocess.run('echo foo'.split())
foo
CompletedProcess(args=['echo', 'foo'], returncode=0)
```

I can't use typical shell features like redirection:

```python
>>> subprocess.run('echo foo > bar'.split())
foo > bar
CompletedProcess(args=['echo', 'foo', '>', 'bar'], returncode=0)
```

I can get around that by explicitly asking for a system shell, and by not splitting up my command:

```python
>>> subprocess.run('echo foo > bar', shell=True)  # creates `bar`!
CompletedProcess(args='echo foo > bar', returncode=0)
```

This doesn't work with cli.Client. If you omit `shell=True`, things are fine:

```python
>>> client.run('echo foo > bar'.split())
CompletedProcess(args=['echo', 'foo', '>', 'bar'], returncode=0, stdout='foo > bar\n', stderr='')
 If you try to execute a command with cli.Client, you'lit'll treat your command string totally wrong:
```

But if you do want the ability to do things like pipe around values, you'll get a `CommandNotFound` exception complaining about the missing command "e":

```python
>>> client.run('echo foo > bar')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/ichimonji10/code/pulp-smash/pulp_smash/cli.py", line 199, in run
    code, stdout, stderr = self.machine[args[0]].run(args[1:], **kwargs)
  File "/home/ichimonji10/.virtualenvs/pulp-smash3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/plumbum/machines/local.py", line 208, in __getitem__
    return LocalCommand(self.which(cmd))
  File "/home/ichimonji10/.virtualenvs/pulp-smash3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/plumbum/machines/local.py", line 178, in which
    raise CommandNotFound(progname, list(cls.env.path))
plumbum.commands.processes.CommandNotFound: ('e', [<LocalPath /home/ichimonji10/.virtualenvs/pulp-smash3/bin>, <LocalPath /usr/local/sbin>, <LocalPath /usr/local/bin>, <LocalPath /usr/bin>, <LocalPath /usr/lib/jvm/default/bin>, <LocalPath /usr/bin/site_perl>, <LocalPath /usr/bin/vendor_perl>, <LocalPath /usr/bin/core_perl>])
```

The core issue is that we're using Plumbum. Plumbum's use case is to provide convenient shell-like combinators by overriding a bunch of Python built-ins, like so:

```python
from plumbum import local
from plumbum.cmd import wc, ls, echo, grep
from plumbum.utils import copy, delete

delete(local.cwd // "*/*.pyc")
for fn in local.cwd / "src" // "*.py":
    print wc("-l", fn)
num_of_src_lines = (ls["-l"] | grep["\\.py"] | wc["-l"])()
(echo["1"] > "/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward")()
```

I've not been doing that. I've been using it because it offers an `SshMachine` class, and that's it.

What's the consequence? The consequence is that I cannot write certain kinds of CLI tests. For example, this is an absolutely necessary sequence of commands to test pulp-admin's errata creation functionality:

```sh
pkglist_csv=$(mktemp)
echo bear,4.1,1,0,noarch,... > "$pkglist_csv"
```

But that requires shell redirection, which cannot be accomplished with `cli.Client`.

Let's drop Plumbum and make `cli.Client` interface with `subprocess.run` ourselves. Well, that's what I'd like to do. But that means dropping support for Python 2.

issue_title: Generate Input Arguments from SQLAlchemy Class?
issue_body:
Hello,
Do you know if it's possible to generate input arguments dynamically from the SQLAlchemy class that will be transformed by the mutation?
Example:

My input arguments for a `CreatePerson` mutation look like this:

```python
class CreatePersonInput(graphene.InputObjectType):
    """Arguments to create a person."""
    name = graphene.String(required=True, description="Name of the person to be created.")
    height = graphene.String(default_value="unknown", description="Height of the person to be created.")
    mass = graphene.String(default_value="unknown", description="Mass of the person to be created.")
    hair_color = graphene.String(default_value="unknown", description="Hair color of the person to be created.")
    skin_color = graphene.String(default_value="unknown", description="Skin color of the person to be created.")
    eye_color = graphene.String(default_value="unknown", description="Eye color of the person to be created.")
    birth_year = graphene.String(default_value="unknown", description="Birth year of the person to be created.")
    gender = graphene.String(default_value="unknown", description="Gender of the person to be created.")
    planet_id = graphene.ID(default_value="unknown", description="Global Id of the planet from which the person to be created comes from.")
    url = graphene.String(default_value="unknown", description="URL of the person in the Star Wars API.")


class CreatePerson(graphene.Mutation):
    """Mutation to create a person."""
    person = graphene.Field(lambda: People, description="Person created by this mutation.")

    class Arguments:
        input = CreatePersonInput(required=True)

...
```

In the meantime, the input arguments for my `UpdatePerson` mutation look like this:

```python
class UpdatePersonInput(graphene.InputObjectType):
    """Arguments to update a person."""
    id = graphene.ID(required=True)
    name = graphene.String()
    height = graphene.String()
    mass = graphene.String()
    hair_color = graphene.String()
    skin_color = graphene.String()
    eye_color = graphene.String()
    birth_year = graphene.String()
    gender = graphene.String()
    planet_id = graphene.ID()
    url = graphene.String()


class UpdatePerson(graphene.Mutation):
    """Update a person."""
    person = graphene.Field(lambda: People, description="Person updated by this mutation.")

    class Arguments:
        input = UpdatePersonInput(required=True)

...
```

Finally, my SQLAlchemy class look like this:

```python
class ModelPeople(Base):
    """People model."""

    __tablename__ = 'people'

    id = Column('id', Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column('name', String)
    height = Column('height', String)
    mass = Column('mass', String)
    hair_color = Column('hair_color', String)
    skin_color = Column('skin_color', String)
    eye_color = Column('eye_color', String)
    birth_year = Column('birth_year', String)
    gender = Column('gender', String)
    planet_id = Column('planet_id', Integer, ForeignKey('planet.id'))
    created = Column('created', String)
    edited = Column('edited', String)
    url = Column('url', String)

...
```

This is all pretty redundant and it would be ideal if we could just reuse the SQLAlchemy class attributes in the `InputObjectType`

it is
```
weights = weights[:-tail_size][:vocabulary_max]
words = words[sorter[:-tail_size]][:vocabulary_max]

And it should be

words = words[sorter[:len(words)-tail_size]][:vocabulary_max]

issue_title: Tensorboard delete option for runs

Since many of us train with Tensorflow on a remote server and use Tensorboard to monitor and evaluate the training I think it would be useful to add a delete option for specific runs on the Tensorboard dashboard.
When I debug a training process I monitor what the network is doing and sometimes restart the process very quickly. Like this my data directory easily gets filled with a lot of fail runs. Deleting them from command is a little tedious cause you always have to check in Tensorboard which ones you might want to keep. And usually you have to close Tensorboard to be able to delete them at all.

Maybe you guys have a suggestion for an improved workflow! Otherwise I think being able to delete runs directly from Tensorboard would be a nice addition. As part of this an 'active' flag for whether a run is still running might be interesting as well. Obviously this flag would disable the deletion option.

issue_title: PReLU alphas should be channel not pixel wise
issue_body:
I think the current implementation of the PReLU layer initializes its alpha parameters for each pixel but as shown in the corresponding [paper](http://arxiv.org/pdf/1502.01852v1.pdf) it should be channel wise. Pixel wise initialization also restricts the input size during inference. 

If this is indeed an error I am happy to submit a pull request.

issue_title: Question about the "Advanced readers: skipgram versus cbow" part of the tutorial.
issue_body: Hello and thank you for this awesome library! Looking over the cool new tutorial and noticed something that made me pause for a bit. When discussing the difference between CBOW and skipgram, the tutorial states: > The skipgram model learns to predict a target word thanks to a nearby word. On the other hand, the cbow model predicts the target word according to its context. The context is represented as a bag of the words contained in a fixed size window around the target word. ![cbo_vs_skipgram](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/13853804/25653118/cb7aa9d4-2feb-11e7-8924-e5e03437bf42.png) Maybe I am misunderstanding, but to me this seems a little bit misleading. I totally agree with the description of CBOW, but my understanding of skipgram was that it tries to predict the context words around the same input word, as illustrated in the paper by Mikolov et al. (Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space): Therefore, in the case of the leather jacket the input would always be "fine", but we are trying to predict "selling", "these", etc. All the best, Arnor

issue_title: Index error when using fit_transform
issue_body:
I am doing text classification on sentiment analysis, where I have a large training set and test set
```
test_data_df.shape (46346, 2) 
train_data_df.shape  (69518, 2)
```

The first column of train_data_df is label, which has value 1 if email is a personal attack and 0 if not. The second column of train_data_df is comment, which is the email content. However, when I try to use     fit_transform      to transform corpus into feature vectors, I got

`
IndexError         Traceback (most recent call last)
`

Below is the code, which I learnt from https://www.codementor.io/jadianes/data-science-python-r-sentiment-classification-machine-learning-du107otfg. Could anyone help me to point out where I might possibly went wrong? Thank you very much!

```
import numpy as np 
import re, nltk
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer       
from nltk.stem.porter import PorterStemmer
test_data_df = pd.read_csv('test.tsv', delimiter="\t", quoting=3)
train_data_df = pd.read_csv('train.tsv', delimiter="\t", quoting=3)
stemmer = PorterStemmer()

def stem_tokens(tokens, stemmer):
       stemmed = []
       for item in tokens:
       stemmed.append(stemmer.stem(item))
       return stemmed

def tokenize(comment):
      comment = re.sub("[^a-zA-Z]", " ", comment)
      tokens = nltk.word_tokenize(comment)
      stems = stem_tokens(tokens, stemmer)
      return stems

vectorizer = CountVectorizer(
      analyzer = 'word',
      tokenizer = tokenize,
      lowercase = True,
      stop_words = 'english',
      max_features = 200
)
corpus_data_features = 
vectorizer.fit_transform(train_data_df.comment.tolist() +  test_data_df.comment.tolist())
```


The last line of code is where problem comes from. Thank you!

issue_title: Bug: dialect parameter has no effect when using %%sql --module ..
issue_body:
[An issue was reported on StackOverflow](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/41944649/datalab-how-to-export-big-query-standard-sql-query-to-dataframe) related to the dialect parameter not being honored when using `%%sql --module ...`. This feels like a bug.

The `-d standard` which is used to set the BigQuery dialect to Standard SQL has no effect in the following example:
``
%%sql --module data_name -d standard
``

Currently users still need to specify the dialect parameter when calling `bq.Query.to_dataframe()` which could be confusing.
```
bq.Query(data_name).to_dataframe(dialect='standard')
```

The following works as expected (i.e. when `--module` is not used in `%%sql`)
``
%%sql -d standard
<sql query>
``

issue_body:
## Detailed Description
The current chart implementation rely on the Chart.js 1.0 which is not maintained anymore.
It lacks some previously used chart type (stacked chart...)

## Context
Upgrading to Chart.js 2.0 will provides support, missing charts, the ability to remove some unmaintained depency for extra charts which are now builtins.

Another possibility is to use Vue.js to draw raw svg.

issue_title: %shared magic or %sos_options --shared
issue_body:
We already have explicit data exchange using magics `%get` and `%put`, and implicit data exchange of variables with names starting with `sos`. Just for users who like to use implicit data exchange but hate the `sos` prefix, perhaps we can provide a way to allow users to specify variables to be shared? We could either make it a list of variables or make it a pattern (e.g. `sos*` to include the default case), make it a dedicated magic `%shared` or an option for `sos` (through `%sos_options`.

Implementation wise this is not very difficult but we need to refrain from providing two much features that might back-fire later.

issue_title: Automatically create cache directories for pretrained embeddings
issue_body: When you try to load your own pretrained embeddings - using eg. ```torchtext.vocab.Vectors``` - if the ```.vector_cache``` directory/the directory in the args doesn't exist, torch's save routine throws an exception. It seems to be pretty simple to just check whether the cache directory exists or not, though, and create it if it doesn't.

Native run command with timeout truncates output
issue_body:
**Testcase**: ssh into a host 'x' and cat a file of ~ 6000 lines
    failure: when we add timeout in run_command
    success: when we remove timeout 

**Possible Contributing Factor**: ssh via wan link with firewall in there [ Local LAN seems to work well ]

_Firewall can be a factor but then it does work without timeout value in run_command or for that matter direct ssh._ _What changes with timeout value on which can impact / truncate output in this case over WAN link [ stable and battle tested ]_ ??

Sample Code:
```

from pprint import pprint
from pssh.utils import enable_host_logger
from pssh.pssh2_client import ParallelSSHClient


hosts=['x']
client = ParallelSSHClient(hosts)
output = client.run_command('cat /tmp/yo', timeout=15)

for host, host_out in output.items():
    try:
        for line in host_out.stdout:
            print 'output',line
        for line in host_out.stderr:
            print 'error',line
    except:
        print 'error'

```

Output: 'error' 

_[ It does read some 500-600 lines and then errors out ]_

Same Code without timeout:

 
```
from pprint import pprint
from pssh.utils import enable_host_logger
from pssh.pssh2_client import ParallelSSHClient


hosts=['x']
client = ParallelSSHClient(hosts)
output = client.run_command('cat /tmp/yo')
timeout=15)

for host, host_out in output.items():
    try:
        for line in host_out.stdout:
            print 'output',line
        for line in host_out.stderr:
            print 'error',line
    except:
        print 'error'
```
Output: As expected  , all 6000 lines in matter of second.

issue_title: numpy is listed in setup.py as a requirement but isn't really a requirement
issue_body:
I had to install websockify on my OpenWRT router, but that failed when it tried to build numpy. Looking at the code, numpy is optional. I removed numpy from install_requires in setup.py and it installed and runs fine.

From https://packaging.python.org/discussions/install-requires-vs-requirements/

> install_requires is a setuptools setup.py keyword that should be used to specify what a project **minimally** needs to run correctly. When the project is installed by pip, this is the specification that is used to install its dependencies.

issue_title: introduce a canonical data dir
issue_body:
Currently when we clone a new tensorlayer repo and run some tutorials, TL will download the data/weights and save them in the current directory. If we run commands like `git clean -fdx`, the data will be lost. If we have two TL repos on the same computer (not sure if this is a common case for many users), the data can't be shared.
I think we can store all the data/weight files in a directory called `DATA_DIR`, it is located at a fix path on a computer, e.g. `~/var/tensorlayer`, `~/.tensorlayer` (can be controlled by an environment variable), and we could organise the well known files as:

```
# DATA_DIR/data/<dataset-name>/<file>
# DATA_DIR/models/<model-name>/<file>
DATA_DIR/
   data/
      mnist/
         t10k-images-idx3-ubyte.gz
         ...
      cifat/
        cifar-10-binary.tar.gz
        ...
   models/
      vgg/
         vgg16_weights.npz
         vgg19_weights.npz
         ...
      yolo/
       
```
